title: The AI honeymoon appears over amid stock sell-off
excerpt: The artificial intelligence spending spree sparked by the release of ChatGPT 3.5 in November 2022 has been unprecedented in its scale. Global AI infrastructu...
content_html:
Tony Owusu

Tue, July 21, 2026 at 1:33 PM CDT 5 min read

The artificial intelligence spending spree sparked by the release of ChatGPT 3.5 in November 2022 has been unprecedented in its scale.

Global AI infrastructure spending is expected to reach $758 billion by 2029, according to the International Data Corporation. That's more than double the $300 billion spent in 2025.

"The AI bubble isn't a result of any actual return on investment," frequent AI critic Ed Zitron wrote in a recent scathing takedown of the industry. He called the outsized AI spending and the economic bubble it has inflated "the greatest capital misallocation in history."

ChatGPT parent company OpenAI has already declared that it intends to spend more than $852 billion by the end of 2030. It intends to spend $50 billion or more on compute power just this year, which, according to Zitron's math, is more than 50% of all global AI compute spend.

For comparison, Chipotle's entire market cap is about $42 billion. But Chipotle also generated about $11 billion in revenue last year, compared to OpenAI's 13.1 billion in revenue.

That incongruity was behind some of the tech sell-off Wall Street witnessed last week.

Big tech AI spending runs into investor resistance

Last week, investors made major pullbacks in chip stocks, leading to a broader sell-off in tech stocks. Information technology was the worst-performing group in the S&P 500 Index last week, falling 1.6%. Meanwhile, the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 Index also lost 4.1%.

Investor concern about runaway AI spending with little return on investment is behind the selloff, analysts told Bloomberg this week.

"Investors are getting to the point where they're uncomfortable with how much money is being spent, and they're worried about a bubble," said Jake Seltz, portfolio manager at Allspring Global Investments. "Ultimately, we need to see a re-acceleration in revenue."

OpenAI has the most popular consumer-facing AI product, with ChatGPT boasting more than 900 million weekly users. But even as the company's revenue has jumped from $2 billion in 2023 to a projected $29 billion in 2026, the company is also losing market share and money on every new user that utilizes its platform.

OpenAI lost about $5 billion in 2024; it expects to lose about $14 billion this year, but outside watchers estimate it will lose as much as $36.6 billion in 2026.

"OpenAI's collapse will be a direct result of its loss-laden economics — its doomed, loss-making subscriptions, its pathetic advertising revenue, and API costs that became a 'huge issue' for its enterprise customers — and the fact that outside of the hype, AI lacks measurable ROI," Zitron says.

sentences.zh: Zitron 表示：“OpenAI 的崩溃将是其亏损严重的经济状况的直接结果——注定亏损的订阅、可怜的广告收入以及对其企业客户来说成为‘大问题’的 API 成本，以及除了炒作之外，人工智能缺乏可衡量的投资回报率这一事实。”

content_html_zh:
托尼·奥乌苏

2026 年 7 月 21 日星期二下午 1:33 CDT 阅读时间 5 分钟

2022 年 11 月 ChatGPT 3.5 的发布引发了人工智能消费热潮，规模空前。

据国际数据公司称，到 2029 年，全球人工智能基础设施支出预计将达到 7580 亿美元。 这比 2025 年花费的 3000 亿美元增加了一倍多。

“人工智能泡沫并不是任何实际投资回报的结果，”频繁的人工智能评论家 Ed Zitron 在最近对该行业的严厉批评中写道。 他称巨额的人工智能支出及其引发的经济泡沫是“历史上最大的资本错配”。

ChatGPT 母公司 OpenAI 已宣布，计划到 2030 年底斥资超过 8520 亿美元。 今年，它打算在计算能力上投入 500 亿美元或更多，根据 Zitron 的计算，这占全球人工智能计算支出的 50% 以上。

相比之下，Chipotle 的总市值约为 420 亿美元。 但 Chipotle 去年也创造了约 110 亿美元的收入，而 OpenAI 的收入为 131 亿美元。

这种不一致是上周华尔街科技股遭遇抛售的原因之一。

大型科技公司人工智能支出遭遇投资者抵制

上周，投资者大幅回调芯片股，导致科技股出现更广泛的抛售。 信息技术是上周标普 500 指数中表现最差的板块，下跌 1.6%。 与此同时，以科技股为主的纳斯达克100指数也下跌了4.1%。

分析师本周向彭博社表示，投资者对人工智能支出失控且投资回报率极低的担忧是此次抛售背后的原因。

Allspring Global Investments 的投资组合经理杰克·塞尔茨 (Jake Seltz) 表示：“投资者对所花的资金感到不安，他们担心泡沫。” “最终，我们需要看到收入重新加速。”

OpenAI 拥有最受欢迎的面向消费者的人工智能产品，ChatGPT 拥有超过 9 亿的每周用户。 但即使该公司的收入从 2023 年的 20 亿美元跃升至 2026 年预计的 290 亿美元，该公司也在失去市场份额，并因每个使用其平台的新用户而损失金钱。

OpenAI 2024 年损失约 50 亿美元；该公司预计今年将损失约 140 亿美元，但外部观察人士估计，到 2026 年，该公司的损失将高达 366 亿美元。

Zitron 表示：“OpenAI 的崩溃将是其亏损严重的经济状况的直接结果——注定亏损的订阅、可怜的广告收入以及对其企业客户来说成为‘大问题’的 API 成本，以及除了炒作之外，人工智能缺乏可衡量的投资回报率这一事实。”
